response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r2c0 is in critical distress: SoC of 10.62 kWh (27.2% of 39 kWh capacity) with a 1.0 kW load means only ~10.6 hours of autonomy remain before complete depletion. The islanded state confirms no grid support. All recent peers (r2c1, r3c0, r1c0) have rejected outbound sharing requests, citing their own survival thresholds and cluster-wide scarcity. Notably, r3c0 has explicitly distrusted r2c0 and prioritizes r4c0 as a reciprocal ally. The repeated pattern of urgent and normal requests from r2c1 and r1c0 suggests they too are in distress but unwilling to support r2c0 given the energy deficit. With no trust circles established and facing rejection from all neighbors, r2c0 must adopt an extremely conservative posture: cease all outbound sharing immediately to preserve the narrow survival window, signal critical urgency to any potential inbound sources, and avoid further antagonizing peers by respecting their rejection rationale. The fallback geographic round-robin policy has proven ineffective; a survival-first stance is now mandatory.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r3c0]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Critical SoC (27.2%); islanded; ~10.6h autonomy. All peers rejecting outbound. Cluster-wide scarcity confirmed. Halt all sharing; focus on inbound requests only. r3c0 explicitly distrusted and prioritizes r4c0."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
